Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Carpal Tunnel and chanting

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2008-01-07 · 3 answers
Hare Krishna,

My wife has developed carpal tunnel in her right wrist. This is causing a lot of pain and problems using her right hand and arm. Would it be ok for her to chant with a clicker? And if it gets worse or she has surgery could she use the clicker in her left hand?

This is really creating some distress for her.

Hari Bhakta dasa
user [179] · 2008-01-07
Hare Krsna !

well mainly as i figured out that a carpal is something like disease is is like, so i think i really can not even say what a clicker is as i think it is sometimes the japa if it is a problem causing distress for chanting or even doing anything like Japa i dont know if she could still chant for a while with her left i can not tell but sometimes the japa is round the neck with its clicket and the arm can rest up in there while chanting.. is this if is it you tell us or me specially as i am also trying to figure what a clicket might be ...

i hope everything is going fine allright.

Hari bol !
user [166] · 2008-01-08
I think its OK to use a clicker in this case if it helps to ease the pain....using the left hand is no problem I think, its not Tulasi.
user [33] · 2008-01-08
Thank you prabhu.
